steps.title: Link renewal to duration
steps.content: The study says subscribers often stay enrolled because automatic payments make them inattentive. When they must actively renew, they are much more likely to discontinue. A rule requiring manual payment would therefore make subscriptions end sooner on average.

steps.title: Exclude unsupported effects
steps.content: A misstates the effect on inattentive consumers, B invents faster declines in service value, and C assumes the affected subscribers are already dissatisfied. The supported prediction is shorter average subscription duration, with remaining long-term subscribers more likely to be actively choosing the service. The answer is D.
